---
format:
  html:
    footnotes-hover: false
    css: styles.css
mainfont: "GT-America"
linkcolor: "#00355a"
anchor-sections: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<head>
<img src='https://csg-state-violent-crime.netlify.app/img/csgjc-logo.png' style='display: none;'> 
</head>

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(csgjcr)
library(highcharter)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(reactable)
library(reactablefmtr)

source("R/utils.R")
```

```{r}
options(scipen=999)


# path to data folder on sharepoint
sp_data_to_plot_path <-
  csg_sp_path("ad_hoc_requests/state_violent_crime_marshall/data")

state_name <- "United States"
state_name_text <- "the United States"

# read in crime data
# srs_us <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_us.rds")) |>
#   filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2012)
# 
# srs_by_cat_us <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_by_cat_us.rds")) |>
#   filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2012)

srs_state <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_state.rds"))

srs_by_cat_us <- srs_state |>
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2013) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarize(
    across(c(pop_total, incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved), \(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) |>
  mutate(
    pop_total = pop_total / 4,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    # pct_unsolved = incidents_unsolved / incidents_reported,
    incidents_solved = incidents_reported - incidents_unsolved,
    pct_solved = incidents_solved / incidents_reported
  )

srs_us <- srs_state |>
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2013) |>
  group_by(year, group) |>
  summarize(
    across(c(pop_total, incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved), \(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pop_total = pop_total,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    # pct_unsolved = incidents_unsolved / incidents_reported
    incidents_solved = incidents_reported - incidents_unsolved,
    pct_solved = incidents_solved / incidents_reported
  )

### import state-level file for mapping
### aggregate counts and create rates
### only need 2019 and 2023 (to calculate change between the two)
### keeping values from each year for tooltips
srs_state_to_map <- srs_state |>
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year %in% c(2019, 2023)) |>
  group_by(state_name, state_abbr, pop_total, year) |>
  summarize(
    n_reported = sum(incidents_reported, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_cleared = sum(incidents_cleared, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_unsolved = sum(incidents_unsolved, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ### calculate rates
  mutate(
    solved_rate = n_cleared / n_reported,
    # unsolved_rate = n_unsolved / n_reported,
    viol_crime_rate_100k = (n_reported / pop_total) * 100000
  ) |>
  group_by(state_name) |>
  ### currently multiplying rate by 100 instead of using scales::percent
  ### may be better way to format within hc
  ### calculate change over time
  mutate(
    n_reported_2019 = n_reported[year == 2019],
    n_reported_2023 = n_reported[year == 2023],
    n_solved_2019 = n_cleared[year == 2019],
    n_solved_2023 = n_cleared[year == 2023],
    viol_crime_rate_100k_2019 = viol_crime_rate_100k[year == 2019],
    viol_crime_rate_100k_2023 = viol_crime_rate_100k[year == 2023],
    solved_rate_2019 = solved_rate[year == 2019] * 100,
    solved_rate_2023 = solved_rate[year == 2023] * 100,
    solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023 = solved_rate_2023 - solved_rate_2019,
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023 = ((viol_crime_rate_100k[year ==
                                                                        2023] - viol_crime_rate_100k[year == 2019]) / viol_crime_rate_100k[year ==
                                                                                                                                             2019]
    ) * 100
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ### now drop 2019
  ### and keep population from 2023
  filter(year == 2023)


min_max_viol_crime <- srs_by_cat_us |>
  filter(year %in% c(min(year), max(year))) |>
  arrange(year)

max_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[2]
min_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[1]

pct_change <-
  (min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[2] - min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[1]) / min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[1]

change_text <- case_when(
  pct_change > 0.005  ~ paste0(percent(abs(pct_change), 1),
                               " more"),
  pct_change < -0.005 ~ paste0(percent(abs(pct_change), 1),
                               " fewer"),
  TRUE                ~ "nearly the same number of"
)

pct_change_solved <-
  min_max_viol_crime$pct_solved[2] - min_max_viol_crime$pct_solved[1]

change_text_solved <- case_when(
  pct_change_solved > 0.005 &
    pct_change_solved < 0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(
      pct_change_solved * 100
    ), 1),
    " percentage point higher than"),
  pct_change_solved >= 0.015  ~ paste0(comma(abs(
    pct_change_solved * 100
  ), 1),
  " percentage points higher than"),
  pct_change_solved < -0.005 &
    pct_change_solved > -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(
      pct_change_solved * 100
    ), 1),
    " percentage point lower than"),
  pct_change_solved <= -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(
    pct_change_solved * 100
  ), 1),
  " percentage points lower than"),
  TRUE                      ~ "nearly the same as"
)

max_viol_crime_off <- srs_us |>
  filter(year == max(year)) |>
  arrange(desc(incidents_reported)) |>
  mutate(pct = incidents_reported / sum(incidents_reported))

### import and prep hex map geo
hex <- sf::read_sf("us_states_hexgrid.geojson") |>
  select(state_abb = iso3166_2)

hex_gj <- hex |>
  sf::st_transform(3857) |>
  geojsonsf::sf_geojson() |>
  jsonlite::fromJSON(simplifyVector = FALSE)

axis_expansion_mult <- 0.5
```

# Trends in Violent Crime and Accountability

## 50-state violent crime data

::: {.panel-tabset}

## 2019–2023 change

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

# Assign colors
darkorange  <- "#7B3014"
orange      <- "#D25E2D"
lightorange <- "#EDB799"
white       <- "#FFFFFF"
lightblue   <- "#9ed4ef"
regblue     <- "#007392"
darkblue    <- "#00475d"

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_2 <- srs_state_to_map |>
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023_clean = as.numeric(comma(
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023, 1
    )),
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023_clean = sprintf("%+3d%%", viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023_clean),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, "</b><br>",
      "Change in violent crime rate: ",
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023_clean,
      "<br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k (2023): ",
      comma(viol_crime_rate_100k_2023, 1),
      "<br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k (2019): ",
      comma(viol_crime_rate_100k_2019, 1)
    ),
    url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abbr))
  )

# Get data range
data_range <-
  range(srs_state_to_map_2$viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023,
        na.rm = TRUE)
min <- data_range[1]
max <- data_range[2]
# print(min); print(max)

# Adjust extremes so they are balanced at zero
new_min <- min(-abs(min), -abs(max))
new_max <- max(abs(min), abs(max))
# print(new_min); print(new_max)

### highchart
highchart() |>
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_2,
    joinBy = c("state_abb", "state_abbr"),
    value = "viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    dataLabels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      format = "{point.state_abb}",
      style = list(
        fontSize = "13px",
        fontFamily = default_fonts,
        fontWeight = 700,
        textOutline = 0
      )
    ),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, percent change: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Change in violent crime, 2019–2023",
    align = "left",
    style = list(
      fontFamily = header_font,
      color = "#005FAD",
      fontSize = "24px",
      fontWeight = "bold"
    )
  ) |>
  hc_subtitle(
    text = "Percent change in crime reported to police per 100,000 residents",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                                 color = "#666666")
  ) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(
    min = new_min,
    max = new_max,
    endOnTick = TRUE,
    startOnTick = TRUE,
    stops = color_stops(
      7,
      c(
        darkblue,
        regblue,
        lightblue,
        white,
        lightorange,
        orange,
        darkorange
      )
    ),
    labels = list(
      format = "{value}%",
      style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                  fontFamily = default_fonts)
    )
  ) |>
  hc_legend(
    align = "center",
    horizontalAlign = "bottom",
    layout = "horizontal",
    # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
    symbolHeight = 25,
    symbolWidth = 250,
    x = 0,
    y = 0
  ) |>
  hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
  )  |>
   hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer",
      accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)),
      point = list(
          events = list(
            click = JS("function(){window.top.location.href = this.options.url}")))
      )
    ) |>  
  hc_caption(
    text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
    align = "right",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |>
  hc_setup()


```

## 2023 crime rate

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

### choose blues from csgjcr for now
blue1 <- "#d4e9f8"
blue2 <- "#9ed4ef"
blue4 <- "#007392"
blue5 <- "#00475d"

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_1 <- srs_state_to_map |> 
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023_clean = as.numeric(comma(viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023,1)),
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023_clean = sprintf("%+3d%%", viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023_clean),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – 2023", "</b><br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k: ", comma(viol_crime_rate_100k_2023, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(n_reported_2023, 1), "<br>"
      ),
    url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abbr))
    )

### highchart 
highchart() |>
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_1,
    joinBy = c("state_abb","state_abbr"),
    value = "viol_crime_rate_100k_2023",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    dataLabels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      format = "{point.state_abb}",
      style = list(
        fontSize = "13px",
        fontFamily = default_fonts,
        fontWeight = 700,
        textOutline = 0
        )
      ),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, violent crime rate: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )
    ) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Violent crime rate, 2023",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = header_font, color = "#005FAD",
                              fontSize = "24px", fontWeight = "bold")
    ) |> 
  hc_subtitle(
    text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                                 color = "#666666")
  ) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(
    endOnTick = TRUE,
    startOnTick = TRUE,
    stops = color_stops(4, c(blue1, blue2, blue4, blue5)),
    labels = list(
      style = list(fontSize = "12px", fontFamily = default_fonts),
      format = "{value:,.0f}"
      )
    ) |>
  hc_legend(
    align = "center",
    horizontalAlign = "bottom",
    layout = "horizontal",
    symbolHeight = 25,
    symbolWidth = 250,
    x = 0,
    y = 0
    ) |>
  hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
    )  |>
   hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer",
      accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)),
      point = list(
          events = list(
            click = JS("function(){window.top.location.href = this.options.url}")))
      )
    ) |>
  hc_caption(
    text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
    align = "right",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |>
  hc_setup()

```

:::

From 2019 to 2023, there were important variations in violent crime trends by offense. Nationally, rates of homicide and aggravated assault increased, while rates of rape and robbery declined.  

Trends in violent crime also varied by state. Between 2019 and 2023, violent crime rates in 32 states decreased, while violent crime rates in 18 states and the District of Columbia increased.


::: {.panel-tabset}

## 2019–2023 change

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

### create object with states to exclude
states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz <- c("Illinois")

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_4 <- srs_state_to_map |>
   ### filter out states in states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz
  mutate(solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023 = ifelse(state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                                     NA,solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023)) |> 
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
     solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023_clean = as.numeric(comma(solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023,1)),
    solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023_clean = sprintf("%+3d", solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023_clean),
    tooltip = ifelse(!state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                     paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, "</b><br>",
      "Change in solved rate: ",
      solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023_clean, "<br>",
      "Solved rate (2023): ", comma(solved_rate_2023, 1), "%", "<br>",
      "Solved rate (2019): ", comma(solved_rate_2019, 1), "%", "<br>"
    ),
      paste0(
      state_name, " did not report enough data", "<br>",
      "on solved crime to the FBI to", "<br>",
      "make reliable estimates."
      )
    ),
    url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abbr)))
  

# Get data range
data_range <-
  range(srs_state_to_map_4$solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023,
        na.rm = TRUE)
min <- data_range[1]
max <- data_range[2]
# print(min); print(max)

# Adjust extremes so they are balanced at zero
new_min <- min(-abs(min), -abs(max))
new_max <- max(abs(min), abs(max))
# print(new_min); print(new_max)

### highchart
highchart() |>
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_4,
    joinBy = c("state_abb", "state_abbr"),
    value = "solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    nullInteraction = TRUE,
    dataLabels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      format = "{point.state_abb}",
      style = list(
        fontSize = "13px",
        fontFamily = default_fonts,
        fontWeight = 700,
        textOutline = 0
      )
    ),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, percent point change: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Change in solved violent crime, 2019–2023",
    align = "left",
    style = list(
      fontFamily = header_font,
      color = "#005FAD",
      fontSize = "24px",
      fontWeight = "bold"
    )
  )  |> 
  hc_subtitle(
        text = "Percentage point change in rate of solved violent crimes from 2019 to 2023",
        align = "left",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                     color = "#666666")
    ) |>
  hc_colorAxis(
    min = new_min,
    max = new_max,
    endOnTick = TRUE,
    startOnTick = TRUE,
    stops = color_stops(
      7,
      rev(c(darkblue, regblue, lightblue, white, lightorange, orange, darkorange))
    ),
    labels = list(
      format = "{value}",
      style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                   fontFamily = default_fonts)
    )
  ) |>
  hc_legend(
    align = "center",
    horizontalAlign = "bottom",
    layout = "horizontal",
    # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
    symbolHeight = 25,
    symbolWidth = 300,
    x = 0,
    y = 0
  ) |>
  hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
  )  |>
   hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer",
      accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)),
      point = list(
          events = list(
            click = JS("function(){window.top.location.href = this.options.url}")))
      )
    ) |>
    hc_caption(
        text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
        align = "right",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |>
  hc_setup()

```

## 2023 solved rate

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_3 <- srs_state_to_map |> 
  ### filter out states in states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz
  # filter(!state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz) |> 
  mutate(solved_rate_2023 = ifelse(state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                                     NA,solved_rate_2023)) |> 
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
    tooltip = ifelse(!state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                     paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – 2023", "</b><br>",
      "Solved rate: ", comma(solved_rate_2023, 1),"%", "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(n_reported_2023, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents solved: ", comma(n_solved_2023, 1)
      ),
      paste0(
      state_name, " did not report enough data", "<br>",
      "on solved crime to the FBI to", "<br>",
      "make reliable estimates."
      )
    ),
    url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abbr)))

### highchart 
highchart() |> 
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_3,
    joinBy = c("state_abb","state_abbr"),
    value = "solved_rate_2023",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    nullInteraction = TRUE,
    dataLabels = list(enabled = TRUE, format = "{point.state_abb}",
                        style = list(fontSize = "13px",
                                     fontFamily = default_fonts,
                                     fontWeight = 700,
                                     textOutline = 0)),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, solved rate: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Solved violent crime, 2023",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = header_font, color = "#005FAD",
                              fontSize = "24px", fontWeight = "bold")
    ) |>   
  hc_subtitle(
        text = "Percentage of violent crimes solved",
        align = "left",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                     color = "#666666")
    ) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(endOnTick = TRUE,
               startOnTick = TRUE,
               stops = color_stops(4, c(blue1,
                                        blue2,
                                        blue4,
                                        blue5)),
               labels = list(format = "{value}%",
                             style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                                          fontFamily = default_fonts))
                             ) |>
  hc_legend(align = "center",
            horizontalAlign = "bottom",
            layout = "horizontal",
            # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
            symbolHeight = 25,
            symbolWidth = 400,
            x = 0,
            y = 0) |> 
     hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
    )  |>
   hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer",
      accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)),
      point = list(
          events = list(
            click = JS("function(){window.top.location.href = this.options.url}")))
      )
    ) |>
    hc_caption(
        text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
        align = "right",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |>
  hc_setup()
```

:::

Between 2019 and 2023, solved violent crime rates in 18 states increased, while solved crime rates in 32 states and the District of Columbia decreased.

Illinois did not report enough data to the FBI on whether crimes were solved to make reliable estimates.

## Reported violent crime trends

In `r max(srs_by_cat_us$year)`, `r srs_by_cat_us |> filter(year == max(year)) |> pull(incidents_reported_rate_total) |> comma(scale = 1e5)` violent crime incidents per 100,000 residents were reported to police in the United States.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_by_cat_us |> 
  mutate(
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5,
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1)
      ),
    state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_setup()
```


```{r}
to_plot <- srs_us |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  group_by(year) |> 
  mutate(tot_crime = sum(incidents_reported)) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pct = incidents_reported / tot_crime,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5,
    group = fct_reorder(group, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Incidents reported per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Total violent incidents reported: ", comma(tot_crime, 1), "<br>",
      "Share of all violent incidents: ", percent(pct, 1)
      )
    ) |> 
  arrange(year, group)
```

## Homicide

```{r}
to_plot_2 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Homicide")

rng <- range(to_plot_2$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_2 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_2$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Homicide incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Aggravated assault

```{r}
to_plot_3 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Aggravated assault")

rng <- range(to_plot_3$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_3 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_3$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Aggravated assault in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where aggravated assault was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Robbery

```{r}
to_plot_5 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Robbery")

rng <- range(to_plot_5$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_5 |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_5$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Robbery in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where robbery was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Rape

```{r}
to_plot_4 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Rape")

rng <- range(to_plot_4$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_4 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_4$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Rape in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where rape was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>  
  hc_setup()
```

## By offense

```{r}
to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    stacking = "normal",
    groupPadding = 0.05,
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text, " by offense")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_legend(reversed = "true") |> 
  hc_setup()
```

:::


## Solved violent crime trends

In `r max(srs_by_cat_us$year)`, `r srs_by_cat_us |> filter(year == max(year)) |> pull(pct_solved) |> percent(suffix = " percent")` of violent crime incidents reported to police in the United States were solved. The percentage of violent crimes that law enforcement solved fell from 2019 to 2022 and increased in 2023 to a level similar to 2020.

```{r}
solved_rate_max <- paste0(comma(min_max_viol_crime$pct_solved[2] * 100, 1), " percent")
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_by_cat_us |> 
  filter(year != 2021) |> 
  mutate(
    pct_solved = pct_solved * 100,
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Solved rate: ", percent(pct_solved / 100, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents solved: ", comma(incidents_solved, 1)
      )
    )


rng <- range(to_plot$pct_solved)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult
y_max <- if_else(y_max > 100, 100, y_max)

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_solved),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, solved rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )     
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste("Solved rate of violent crime in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
    labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%")
  )
```

## By offense

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_us |> 
  filter(year != 2021) |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  mutate(
    pct_solved = pct_solved * 100,
    group = fct_reorder(group, pct_solved, last),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Solved rate: ", percent(pct_solved / 100, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents solved: ", comma(incidents_solved, 1)
      )
    ) |> 
  arrange(desc(year), pct_solved)

# rng <- range(to_plot$pct_solved)
# mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult
# 
# y_min <- rng[1] - mult
# y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
# y_max <- rng[2] + mult
# y_max <- if_else(y_max > 100, 100, y_max)

to_plot |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_solved, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, solved rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )     
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Solved rate of violent crime in ", state_name_text, " by offense")) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
    labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%")
  )
```

:::


## Violent crime and solved crime by state, 2023

```{r}
### function for percent change (thanks Matt!)
add_plus_sign_percent_change <- function(value) {
  
  if (!is.numeric(value)) 
    paste0(value)
  
  if (!is.na(value) & value >= 0)
    paste0("+", round(value,digits = 0),"%")
  
  else
    paste0(round(value,digits=0),"%")

}

add_plus_sign_percent_point_change <- function(value) {
  
  if (!is.numeric(value)) 
    paste0(value)
  
  if (!is.na(value) & value >= 0)
    paste0("+", round(value,digits = 0))
  
  else
    paste0(round(value,digits=0))
}


### create state-level table
srs_state_to_map |> 
    select(
      state_name,
      viol_crime_rate_100k_2023,
      n_reported_2023,
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023,
      solved_rate_2023,
      solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023
      ) |> 
   ### recode all solved incident data to NA if state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz
  mutate(
    across(solved_rate_2023:solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023,
                ~ if_else(state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                          NA, .)
           )
    ) |> 
  reactable_template(
    sort_col = list("state_name" = "asc"),
    columns = list(
      state_name = colDef(name = "State"),
      viol_crime_rate_100k_2023 = colDef(name = "Violent Incidents per 100k (2023)"), 
      n_reported_2023 = colDef(name = "Violent Incidents Reported (2023)"), 
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2023 = colDef(
        name = "Percent change in violent crime rate (2019 to 2023)",
        # format = colFormat(suffix = "%", 
        #                    digits = 0),
        cell = add_plus_sign_percent_change
        ),
      solved_rate_2023 = colDef(
        name = "Solved Rate (2023)",
        format = colFormat(suffix = "%", digits = 0)
        ),
      solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023 = colDef(
        name = "Percentage point change in solved rate (2019 to 2023)",
        # format = colFormat(digits = 0),
        cell = add_plus_sign_percent_point_change,
        )
      )
    )

```
<br>
<br>
Data regarding crime and solved rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr). Violent crime includes homicide, aggravated assault, robbery, and rape. Crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total population for a given geography. Solved rates are the number of reported crimes not cleared by arrest or by exceptional means divided by the number of reported crimes. For more information, see the data sources and methodology section below. 

::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse=true}

## Data sources and methodology

Data regarding crime and solved rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr){target="_parent"}. Counts of reported violent crime between 2013 and 2023 are derived from the Summary Reporting System (SRS) and the National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS). The FBI generated national estimates for reported crime and clearances based on agency-level reports, and these estimates were published through 2023 in the FBI's annual report, [_Crime in the U.S._](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019){target="_parent"} and on the FBI's [Crime Data Explorer](https://cde.ucr.cjis.gov/LATEST/webapp/#/pages/home){target="_parent"}. For agency-level clearance data, we used [Jacob Kaplan's version of the Offenses Known and Clearances by Arrest](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/OESSD1) dataset.

The FBI stopped SRS data collection after data year 2020 and required law enforcement agencies to use NIBRS to report crime statistics to the FBI. This change decreased the number of agencies that submitted their crime data and thus reduced the reliability of state estimates in 2021. In 2023, the FBI allowed agencies who were unable to submit NIBRS data to submit SRS data again. Ninety-four percent of the U.S. population was covered in the 2023 crime data submissions to the FBI, with most states providing close to complete data. However, the population upon which the estimates were derived decreased by over 10 percent in three states: Florida, Hawaii, and Illinois, so conclusions about crime changes in those states should be interpreted with additional caution.

Crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total U.S. population. Population data come from the U.S. Census Bureau [American Community Survey 1-year estimates (table B01003)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/data.html){target="_parent"} and the [2020 Decennial Census Redistricting Data (table P1)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/decennial-census/about/rdo/summary-files.html){target="_parent"}.

Solved rates are the number of reported crimes cleared by arrest or by exceptional means divided by the number of reported crimes. A reported crime incident is [cleared by exceptional means](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019/topic-pages/clearances){target='_parent'} if a law enforcement agency is unable to arrest or formally charge a person with a crime, but the agency has met the following conditions:
    
a) Identified the person who committed the crime
b) Gathered enough evidence to support an arrest, make a charge, and turn over the person to the court for prosecution
c) Identified the suspect’s exact location so that the person could be taken into custody immediately
d) Encountered a circumstance outside the control of law enforcement that prohibits the agency from arresting, charging, and prosecuting the person
    
Examples of exceptional clearances include the death of the person who committed the crime; the victim’s refusal to cooperate with the prosecution after the person who committed the crime has been identified; or the denial of extradition.
:::
